verification_gate: The task family covers one Rust workspace owner, Lua sandbox and policy ABI, dependency audit, cached build, hermetic tests, Ubuntu-first CI, and rollback.

lua_sandbox_and_policy_abi.isolation: one fresh PluginHost per script execution (src-tauri plugin_run)
lua_sandbox_and_policy_abi.feature_gate: plugin-host default-off; enabled only by src-tauri
lua_sandbox_and_policy_abi.ci_assertion: cargo test -p lifeos-core --features plugin-host asserts sandbox tests incl. stripped_globals_list_matches_doc

dependency_audit.tls_policy: rustls-only; ban openssl-sys + native-tls across the graph
dependency_audit.esp32_isolation_assertion: cargo tree -p lifeos-core --features storage | grep openssl-sys must be empty
dependency_audit.source_policy: all crates from crates.io, never the meta-ruvector git monorepo
dependency_audit.license_allowlist: MIT OR Apache-2.0 (declared on every member)

cached_build.cache_strategy: shared target/ + registry/git cache keyed on Cargo.lock hash + toolchain
cached_build.release_exclusion: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl static release keeps default linker for reproducibility

hermetic_tests.db_isolation: storage tests use tempfile dev-dep (no fixed path, no $APPDATA)
hermetic_tests.no_network: reqwest MCP transport behind mcp-http; not exercised in unit tests
hermetic_tests.js_hermeticity: Vitest keeps sendAiMessage canned (no invoke()); lucide-vue-next mock-aliased

added_lanes: dependency-audit gate (cargo-deny + cargo tree openssl-sys isolation)
ubuntu_first_ci.other_os_scope: macOS/Windows out of scope for v0

rollback.mechanism: restore archived build plan (task-family + proof); source workspaces unchanged
rollback.reversibility: plugin-host default-off; new .cargo/config.toml + deny.toml deletable; CI lanes appended and removable
rollback.no_mutation: no Cargo workspace / dependency / profile / product mutation (blocked_paths honored)

flake_and_reproducible_dev_tasks.flake_contract: pins one Rust toolchain (stable >=1.77), provides x86_64-unknown-linux-musl target, vendored-Luau prereqs + mold; DEFERS toolchain/dev-shell ownership to FOUNDATION-001 Yazelix+Nix package

gate_result.store_001_alignment: store-neutral: no work item adds a second durable writer or reclassifies any state class; storage feature flag + no-default-features CI guard + openssl-sys ban preserve the STORE-001 tiering (PostgreSQL canonical durable, redb transient)

proof_summary: FOUNDATION-002 defines the Rust-native application and governed Lua build plan for the consolidated LifeOS application as planning artifacts only. The task family generated/task_families/FOUNDATION-002.json enumerates 8 work items that cover each of the seven gate clauses exactly once, plus the goal's flake/linker/reproducible-dev extras. One Rust workspace owner (A): the repository-root [workspace] manifest (resolver=2) is the sole owner of members src-tauri, crates/lifeos-core, crates/lifeos-daemon and the firmware/esp32 exclusion, with a shared 1.77 MSRV floor. Lua sandbox and policy ABI (B): the governed boundary is Luau via mlua (luau/vendored/send), freezing STRIPPED_GLOBALS = [io, os, package, debug, dofile, loadfile, load], Lua::sandbox(true), the {Compile, Runtime, Sandbox} error taxonomy, one fresh PluginHost per plugin_run call, and the default-off plugin-host feature — all asserted by the existing sandbox tests. Dependency audit (C): a cargo-deny + cargo tree gate that bans openssl-sys/native-tls (rustls-only; the ESP32 `cargo tree | grep openssl-sys` isolation assertion), requires crates.io sourcing over the meta-ruvector monorepo, allowlists MIT OR Apache-2.0, and runs --locked. Cached build (D): shared target/registry cache keyed on Cargo.lock + toolchain and a mold/lld dev linker via a new .cargo/config.toml, explicitly excluded from the reproducible x86_64-unknown-linux-musl static release. Hermetic tests (E): tempfile-scoped DBs, no network (reqwest behind mcp-http), vendored Luau, `cargo test --workspace --locked --offline`, and the Vitest dual-mode canned-AI + lucide mock. Ubuntu-first CI (F): extends the existing ubuntu-latest ci.yml (bun run check) with cargo test --locked, the no-default-features storage guard, the plugin-host lane, the dependency-audit gate, and fmt/clippy — additively, removing no lane. Rollback (G): restore the archived build plan; every surface is additive and reversible and no Cargo/dependency/profile/product mutation occurs. Flake + reproducible dev tasks (H): a flake dev-shell contract pinning one toolchain and the musl target while deferring toolchain ownership to the FOUNDATION-001 Yazelix+Nix package, with bun and cargo tasks running identically in-shell and in CI. Every assignment traces to a named source (Cargo.toml, plugin.rs, ci.yml, CLAUDE.md guards, the Architecture Blueprint, and FOUNDATION-001). The plan is store-neutral and aligned with STORE-001: no second durable writer, tiering preserved via the storage feature flag and the no-default-features guard. No product code, dependency, workspace, or profile was mutated; product_execution_performed is false.
